textPayload:
"explanation": "Al-Qurtubi's tafsir for the concept of 'anger' extends to various manifestations, particularly divine wrath and its consequences, as well as human anger and its regulation. He notes that those who reject Allah's signs and kill prophets, such as the Jews mentioned in the context of their abasement, incur divine anger. This abasement is a direct consequence of their disobedience and transgression beyond limits, signifying a state of divine displeasure. The term 'bā’ū' (brought down) is interpreted as either 'returned' or 'endured' divine anger, emphasizing the inescapable nature of this consequence for persistent wrongdoing.

**Retaliation and Anger Management**
In discussing the principle of retaliation (qisās) in the sacred months (2:194), al-Qurtubi highlights that while transgression permits a proportionate response, it must be tempered by fear of Allah. He clarifies that retaliation should be equal to the transgression, preventing uncontrolled anger from leading to excessive revenge. For instance, an insult may be met with a similar insult, but not with false accusations or attacks on family, underscoring the principle that disobedience cannot be countered by disobedience. This regulation of response implicitly addresses the management of anger, ensuring justice rather than emotional excess. He also discusses the scholarly debate on whether this verse was abrogated by later verses on fighting, with some scholars arguing for its continued applicability in matters of enmity, property crimes, and physical injury within the Muslim community.

**Usury and Divine War**
Al-Qurtubi extensively details the severe divine condemnation of usury (ribā), stating that practitioners will rise from their graves like those driven mad by Shayṭān's touch (2:275). He cites a hadith from the Night Journey describing the physical torment of usurers in the Interspace. The declara

"text_saheeh_international": "And those who believed and did righteous deeds will be admitted to gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding eternally therein by permission of their Lord; and their greeting therein will be, \"Peace!\"",
"arabic_text": "وَأُدْخِلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ تَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيهَا سَلَـٰمٌ"
"explanation": "Al-Qurtubi's tafsir is not available for this verse, as his comprehensive commentary extends only up to Surah 4, Verse 22."

... eternal admission to gardens of bliss, flowing with rivers, all by the explicit permission of their Lord. The verse culminates by highlighting that their perpetual greeting within this abode will be 'Peace,' signifying an environment of complete tranquility and divine favor. This serves as a profound motivation for believers to consistently embody faith and good conduct in their earthly lives."

"arabic_text": "وَأُدْخِلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ تَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيهَا سَلَـٰمٌ"

"explanation": "The provided excerpt from Ibn Kathir's tafsir is not relevant to Surah 14, Verse 23, as it pertains to Surah Al-Qalam (68) and Surah Al-Kahf (18). However, drawing upon the general methodology of classical exegetes like Ibn Kathir, this verse delineates the ultimate felicity awaiting the righteous, contrasting sharply with the fate of the disbelievers described in preceding verses. It emphasizes the dual prerequisite for divine reward: sincere faith (Iman) and righteous actions (Amal Salih).

**The Condition for Entry: Faith and Righteous Deeds**
Classical mufassirun consistently highlight that entry into Paradise is contingent upon both genuine belief in Allah and His messengers, and the performance of good deeds. This dual emphasis, frequently reiterated in the Quran, underscores that faith without action is incomplete, and actions without sincere faith are devoid of true spiritual value. The term 'الصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ' (righteous deeds) encompasses all acts of obedience to Allah, both obligatory and supererogatory, performed with sincerity.

**Description of Paradise**
The verse describes Paradise as 'جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ' (gardens beneath which river
INFO: Query expanded from 'lust' to 'shahwa hawa fitna'

"text_saheeh_international": "Indeed, the example of worldly life is but like rain which We have sent down from the sky that the plants of the earth absorb - [those] from which men and livestock eat - until, when the earth has taken on its adornment and is beautified and its people suppose that they have capability over it, there comes to it Our command by night or by day, and We make it as a harvest [cut down], as if it had not flourished yesterday. Thus do We explain in detail the signs for a people who reflect.",
"arabic_text": "إِنَّمَا مَثَلُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاءٍ أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ مِمَّا يَأْكُلُ النَّاسُ وَالْأَنْعَامُ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَخَذَت

... Kathir and al-Qurtubi emphasize the fleeting nature of worldly attractions and the profound misguidance that results from prioritizing personal whims over divine guidance. The overarching message is to cultivate spiritual vigilance, seek refuge from satanic whispers, and redirect human aspirations towards the eternal rewards of the Hereafter rather than the ephemeral allurements of this world."

Thus do We explain in detail the signs for a people who reflect.",

"arabic_text": "إِنَّمَا مَثَلُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاءٍ أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ مِمَّا يَأْكُلُ النَّاسُ وَالْأَنْعَامُ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَخَذَتِ الْأَرْضُ زُخْرُفَهَا وَازَّيَّنَتْ وَظَنَّ أَهْلُهَا أَنَّهُمْ قَادِرُونَ عَلَيْهَا أَتَاهَا أَمْرُنَا لَيْلًا أَوْ نَهَارًا فَجَعَلْنَاهَا حَصِيدًا كَأَن لَّمْ تَغْنَ بِالْأَمْسِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ"
"text_saheeh_international": "And Allah invites to the Home of Peace [i.e., Paradise] and guides whom He wills to a straight path.",
"arabic_text": "وَاللَّهُ يَدْعُو إِلَىٰ دَارِ السَّلَامِ وَيَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ"

"text_saheeh_international": "But if they do not respond to you, then know that they only follow their [own] desires. And who is more astray than one who follows his desire without guidance from Allah? Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.",

"arabic_text": "فَإِن لَّمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَكَ فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّمَا يَتَّبِعُونَ أَهْوَاءَهُمْ ۚ وَمَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِّنَ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ"
